_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the growing ways that Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples' knowledge and experiences are being recognised — through events like NAIDOC Week, Reconciliation Week, and National Sorry Day.

_source.w: We are learning about the growing ways that Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples' knowledge and experiences are being recognised — through events like NAIDOC Week, Reconciliation Week, and National Sorry Day.
y: These events help all Australians learn about and honour the world's oldest living cultures.
Understanding recognition events shows us that acknowledging the past is part of building a fairer future.
Learning about these occasions helps us participate respectfully and meaningfully.
eg: Finding out what NAIDOC Week stands for and what happens during it.
Learning the story behind National Sorry Day and why it is important.
Discussing what Reconciliation Week means and how our school can take part.
Exploring when the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander flags are flown at special occasions.
Learning about Mabo Day and the significance of Eddie Mabo's story.
Talking about how recognition has changed over time and what more could be done.
